---
title: "House Price Prediction using Boston Housing Dataset in R"
author: "Alekhya Gangopadhyay"
output: word_document
---

# 1. Introduction
House price prediction is an important application of machine learning. In this project, we use the Boston Housing dataset to predict median house prices.

---

# 2. Load Libraries

```{r}
library(MASS)
library(caret)
data(Boston)
head(Boston)
str(Boston)
summary(Boston)
dim(Boston)
colSums(is.na(Boston))
cor(Boston)
plot(Boston$rm, Boston$medv, col="blue", pch=19)
set.seed(123)
trainIndex <- createDataPartition(Boston$medv, p=0.8, list=FALSE)

train <- Boston[trainIndex, ]
test <- Boston[-trainIndex, ]
model <- lm(medv ~ ., data=train)
summary(model)
pred <- predict(model, newdata=test)
head(pred)
actual <- test$medv

rmse <- sqrt(mean((actual - pred)^2))
rmse
plot(actual, pred, col="red", pch=19)
abline(0,1)
